open_question: Why does NHC ||y|| explode at ~1.69-1.79 s?
framing_note: Audit innov is NHC synthetic (z≡0 on body lat/vert), not GNSS. 'Anomalous measurement' does not apply as in GNSS.
first_look: truth v_body lat/vert vs filter v_body (= -innov) in window [1.69, 1.79], ctrl vs latch

A_cascade.claim: Truth v_body lat/vert stays near 0; filter v_body diverges — state already degraded from attitude-Z loop (onset 1.34 → break 1.59 → innov lag).
A_cascade.if_confirmed: Filter/state cascade — continue early-loop line; not a K/P intervention

B_nhc_too_rigid_for_slalom_turn.claim: Truth itself has non-negligible body lateral/vertical velocity in the tight turn — real vehicle physics (sideslip / bank / imperfect non-holonomy), not a filter bug. NHC is penalizing legitimate motion.
B_nhc_too_rigid_for_slalom_turn.falsify_if: Truth ||v_body|| stays small while filter innov explodes
B_nhc_too_rigid_for_slalom_turn.if_confirmed: DESIGN conclusion, not bug hunt: NHC model too rigid for this scenario's turn dynamics. Sensible next lever = relax/gate NHC in that regime — do NOT chase another EKF-internal fault.

do_not: Touch K_bias / path_scale / zero-P before A vs B
If B: reinterpret as 'another bug to hunt'
